name: omarchy_making_your_own_theme
content:
Making your own theme 
You can add your own themes to ~/.config/omarchy/themes. Just copy one of the
existing ones as a base (ook in ~/.local/share/omarchy/themes), then tweak to your
deight. As ong as your theme is inside that foder, it' be incuded in the theme seection
menu.
The main fie you have to tweak is colors.toml . That defines the coor set that's then
used to generate configurations for the termina Ghostty/Aacritty/Kitty), btop, Chromium,
Hyprand, Hyprock, Mako, SwayOSD, Waker, and Waybar.
You can aso use the incuded Aether appication to create a new theme using a ovey GUI
interface to pay with coors and search for backgrounds. Just start it via the app auncher
on Super + Space .
Light mode 
If you're making a ight mode theme, drop an empty fie caed light.mode  in the root of
your theme. Then it' automaticay be paired with ight mode for a the apps.
Icon coors 
If you'd ike to coor-match the fie manager icons to your theme, add a fie caed
icons.theme  with the name of the icon set you want to you. By defaut, the options are:
Yaru Yaru-blue Yaru-dark Yaru-magenta Yaru-olive Yaru-prussiangreen Yaru-purple
Yaru-red Yaru-sage Yaru-wartybrown Yaru-yellow.
Distributing your theme 
If you want to distribute your theme so others can use it, you need to put it on a pubic git
server, ike GitHub. Then peope can insta it using Insta > Theme in the Omarchy menu
using that URL. It's recommended that you foow the naming convention of omarchy-
[themename]-theme, as the theme wi show correcty as just [themename]  in the theme
seection menu after instaation.
You can have your theme added to the extra themes page by pinging @tahayvr on the
#omarchy Discord
